---
title: "Course Schedule"
subtitle: "STAT 184 - Summer 2026"
---

::: callout-note
This schedule is tentative and subject to change. Substantive changes will be announced in class and on Canvas. Class meets Monday through Friday, 9:35 - 10:50 a.m., May 18 - June 26, 2026.

**No class:** Monday May 25 (Memorial Day) · Friday June 19 (Juneteenth).
:::

::: callout-tip
**Reading the table:** DC = *Data Computing* (Kaplan & Beckman), free at <https://dtkaplan.github.io/DataComputingEbook/>. R4DS = *R for Data Science* (Wickham, Çetinkaya-Rundel, Grolemund), free at <https://r4ds.hadley.nz>. Reading the relevant chapter *before* class on Perusall is required; in-class activities assume you've done the reading.
:::

## Week 1 - Foundations (May 18 - 22)

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon May 18 | Course intro · what tidy data is · RStudio tour | [DC §1.1–1.5](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18298036) | — |
| Tue May 19 | R, RStudio, and the console · vectors · data types | [DC §2.1–2.8](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18307706) | — |
| Wed May 20 | Functions, packages, the tidyverse · objects | [DC §3.1–3.5](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18307709) | [HW 0: Setup](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18308171) |
| Thu May 21 | Quarto basics · writing reproducible documents | [DC §4.1–4.7](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18307710) | — |
| Fri May 22 | **Quiz 1** · the pipe and chaining syntax | [DC §3.6](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18307712) | [HW 1: Basic R & Plots](https://psu.instructure.com/courses/2487468/assignments/18311694) · Quiz 1 (in class) |

: Week 1 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

## Week 2 - Tidy Data & ggplot (May 25 - 29)

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon May 25 | **No class - Memorial Day** | — | — |
| Tue May 26 | Data frames · intro to data graphics | [DC §5.1–5.6]() | — |
| Wed May 27 | ggplot2 basics · frames, glyphs, scales, facets | [DC §6.1–6.5]() | [HW 2]() |
| Thu May 28 | Multivariable graphics · specifying graphs · axes & labels | [DC §8.1–8.3]() | — |
| Fri May 29 | **Quiz 2** · graphics critique activity | — | Quiz 2 (in class) |

: Week 2 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

## Week 3 - Data Wrangling (June 1 - 5)

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon Jun 1 | Wrangling concepts · reduction & transformation · `group_by` | [DC §7.1–7.7]() | — |
| Tue Jun 2 | The data verbs: `select`, `filter`, `mutate`, `arrange` | [DC §10.1–10.6]() | [HW 3]() |
| Wed Jun 3 | Deeper practice with `group_by` and `summarize` | (Review Ch 7, 10) | — |
| Thu Jun 4 | Reshaping: `pivot_longer`, `pivot_wider` | [DC §12.1]() | [**Project: Team formation**]() |
| Fri Jun 5 | **Quiz 3** · Git/GitHub for collaboration | [DC §9.1–9.2]() | Quiz 3 (in class) |

: Week 3 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

**Weekend:** Install GitHub Desktop or configure Git locally. By Monday Jun 8, your team should have a working repo with all members able to push.

## Week 4 - Joins, Import, **Exam** (June 8 - 12)

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon Jun 8 | Joining tables: `inner_join`, `left_join`, anti-joins | [DC §11.1–11.3]() | [Reading Quiz 4]() |
| Tue Jun 9 | Importing data: CSV, Excel, factors vs strings, cleaning | [DC §16.1–16.2, 16.4–16.5]() | [HW 4]() |
| Wed Jun 10 | **Exam review** · practice problems in class | — | — |
| **Thu Jun 11** | **EXAM (in-class, on paper, closed book)** | — | — |
| Fri Jun 12 | Light day · web scraping & HTML tables | [DC §16.3]() | [**Project Checkpoint 1**]() |

: Week 4 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

## Week 5 - Functions, Iteration, & Strings (June 15 - 19)

::: callout-note
Week 5 covers material that *Data Computing* doesn't address in depth. Readings for Mon–Wed come from **R for Data Science** (free online).
:::

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon Jun 15 | Writing functions in R | [R4DS Ch 25 (Functions)](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/functions) | — |
| Tue Jun 16 | Control flow · `if/else`, `for`, `while` | [R4DS Ch 26 §26.1–26.3](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/iteration) | [HW 4]() |
| Wed Jun 17 | Vectorized operations · `purrr::map` | [R4DS Ch 26 §26.4–26.5](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/iteration) | — |
| Thu Jun 18 | Strings, dates, and regex | [DC §17.1–17.2]() | **Project oral check-in #1** (during class) |
| Fri Jun 19 | **No class - Juneteenth** · Quiz 5 rescheduled to Mon Jun 22 | — | — |

: Week 5 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

## Week 6 - Special Topics & Project (June 22 - 26)

| Date | Topic | Read before class | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mon Jun 22 | **Quiz 5 (rescheduled)** · unsupervised learning, clustering | [DC §18.2]() | Quiz 5 (in class) |
| Tue Jun 23 | Decision trees / CART | [DC §18.3]() | [HW 5]() |
| Wed Jun 24 | **Project work day** · oral check-in #1 makeups | — | [**Project Checkpoint 2**]() |
| Thu Jun 25 | **Project work day** · oral check-in #2 (final walkthrough) | — | — |
| Fri Jun 26 | **Quiz 6** · short project presentations · course wrap-up | — | Quiz 6 (in class) · [**Final project report due 11:59 p.m.**]() |

: Week 6 schedule {tbl-colwidths="[10,35,25,30]"}

## Submission cheat-sheet

| Item | Format | Where |
|---|---|---|
| Readings | Reading/Discussion | Perusall (Linked on Canvas) |
| Homework | Quarto rendered to PDF or HTML | Canvas |
| Quizzes / Exam | Paper | In class |
| In-class activities | Paper or Canvas | In class or end-of-day Canvas |
| Project | Quarto rendered to PDF + GitHub repo | Canvas |

: Submission cheat-sheet {tbl-colwidths="[25,40,35]"}

## Sources

- **DC** = Kaplan, D. & Beckman, M. (2021). *Data Computing* (2nd ed.). <https://dtkaplan.github.io/DataComputingEbook/>
- **R4DS** = Wickham, H., Çetinkaya-Rundel, M., & Grolemund, G. (2023). *R for Data Science* (2nd ed.). <https://r4ds.hadley.nz>
